BUDGET_RISK_DETAILS:
-   Need to drive more single-game ticket sales and maximize ROAS, especially as the Steph Curry era nears its end and urgency to sell tickets increases.
-   Limited testing and variation in creative; typically receive “one set of assets” from creative, so they’re not meaningfully testing many versions (e.g., 30+ variations).
-   Audience strategy is underdeveloped; they acknowledge they “could probably do a little better” with audience segmentation and matchup-specific audiences, and are interested in untapped segments and insights.
-   Lack of granular creative insight today; agency reporting is focused on high-level performance (ticket sales, ROAS) rather than what specific creative attributes (jersey color, player, background, tone, etc.) are driving performance.
-   Speed-to-market and control: content and optimization live heavily with the agency; teams increasingly want more direct control and faster reaction to big moments or opportunities in-game and around marquee matchups.
-   Complexity across multiple brands (Warriors, Valkyries, Chase Center, etc.) increases operational overhead; smaller brands get less attention despite similar workflows.

- A lack of a mutual plan or clear responsibilities hinders progress and customer alignment with Sharkmob A.

- Minimal engagement and technical exploration leave Adobe's solution disconnected from the customer's challenges and needs.

- A lack of clear urgency and unresolved group-level blockers continue to delay customer decision-making.  
- Persistent technical uncertainties and incomplete milestones undermine customer confidence in the proposed solution.  
- Budget allocation remains uncertain, hindered by global negotiations and unresolved procurement dependencies.

- A lack of clear decision timeline and leadership changes weaken deal momentum and urgency.  
- The absence of a formalized mutual plan hinders alignment on steps, responsibilities, and timelines.  
- Customer uncertainty around technical validation and existing investments complicates solution adoption.

- A fragmented plan lacks customer agreement and alignment with PepsiCo's budgeting processes.  
- The unclear budget allocation and pricing concerns hinder the path to value realization.  
- Limited engagement with decision-makers risks losing influence and alignment on priorities.

- A lack of a formalized mutual plan and clear next steps is stalling deal progression.  
- Absence of urgency and compelling events has delayed decision-making and reduced momentum.  
- Customer hesitates to transition from Pardot, with unclear alignment of Marketo to their needs.

----- Original Message -----
Sent: Wednesday, December 14, 2011 04:02 PM
Subject: DARPA - Adobe LiveCycle DRM Install

Hi All,

I just wanted to inform everyone that we've had a very successful day
today, and we just finished up testing the installation of Adobe LiveCycle
Document Rights Management software.

Doug and I worked with David and Vincent to help install and setup DRM on
a Windows Server 2008 VM, with a Microsoft SQL Server 2008 database. DRM
is also integrated to DARPA's Active Directory (LDAP), so all the users
and groups are available to DRM for assigning users/groups to policies
within DRM.

We went ahead and created sample policies and watermarks, and tested a few
scenarios of protecting documents, enabling/disabling user permissions
(ability to copy, modify or print docs), revoking a document after it has
been distributed, and preventing users from screen captures and pictures
via a custom watermark.

- Aligned SC: N/A
- Solution Summary: Acrobat Sign (with MS Office and SFDC integration).
- Qualification Quality: Medium
- Tech Sale Complete: Y
- Tech Risk: L
- Tech Gap/Blockers: N/A
- Business Risk: L
- BU Support Needed: N
- PSS Align: N

Notes:
Product/ solution demo delivered on 20/04/2022 with 3 use cases: (1) Capex request form, (2) Tax Invoice (MS Outlook integration), and (3) SFDC integration (standard NDA form). 

The key user provided a positive feedback on our great user experience and integration capability.
